__NOTOC__ {{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Nimzowitsch-Larsen, English variation| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd|nd|rd|= |pd|pd| |pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|= | | | | | | | | |= | | |pd| | | | | |= | | | | | | | | |= | |pl| | | | | | |= |pl| |pl|pl|pl|pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= |moves=1. b3 c5 |eco=[[Chess/ECOA|A01]] |parent=[[../|Nimzowitsch-Larsen Attack]] }} ==1...c5 · English variation== '''1...c5''' is a sideline pursued by Black when they assume White will be more booked up with the theory following [[../1...e5|'''1...e5''']] or [[../1...d5|'''1...d5''']]. Having commited to b3 already, White's continuation is invariably '''2. Bb2''', but, since 1...c5 does not control e4 or c4, Black invites White to later play e4 themselves, transposing into a [[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...c5/2. b3|b3 Sicillian]], or c4, transposing into a [[Chess Opening Theory/1. c4/1...c5|Symmetrical English]], which Black hopes to be more comfortable playing. Most commonly, White declines this invitiation and keeps things in Nimzo-Larsen territory with '''3. e3'''. Then, Black can choose to play e5 or d5 with the additional central control afforded by having already played c5. ==Theory table== '''1.b3 c5''' {| border="0" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="4" |- ! ! align="left" | 2 ! align="left" | 3 ! align="left" | 4 ! align="left" | 5 ! align="left" | 6 ! align="left" | 7 ! align="left" | 8 ! align="left" | 9 |- ! align="right" | Nimzowitsch-Larsen Attack,
